    app.run(host='0.0.0.0', port=3000)
    print('✅ Server running on port 3000')
    print('📡 Waiting for shipments from Jenni Logistics...')
Step 2: Send Status Updates to Jenni Logistics Server
Important: After receiving shipments from Jenni Logistics, you need to send status updates back to Jenni Logistics server when delivery status changes (e.g., delivered, postponed, returned). 
# status_updater.py - Send updates to Jenni Logistics server
import requests
from datetime import datetime, timedelta

JENNI_API_URL = 'https://almasara.jenni.delivery/api'
JENNI_USERNAME = 'your_username'
JENNI_PASSWORD = 'your_password'

auth_token = None
token_expiry = None

# ==========================================
# STEP 2: Login to Jenni Logistics
# ==========================================
def login_to_jenni():
    global auth_token, token_expiry
    
    try:
        response = requests.post(
            f'{JENNI_API_URL}/v2/auth/login',
            json={
                'username': JENNI_USERNAME,
                'password': JENNI_PASSWORD
            }
        )
        response.raise_for_status()
        data = response.json()
        
        auth_token = data['token']
        token_expiry = datetime.now() + timedelta(seconds=data['expires_in'])
        
        print('✅ Logged in to Jenni Logistics successfully')
        return auth_token
    except Exception as error:
        print(f'❌ Login failed: {error}')
        raise

def ensure_valid_token():
    """Ensure token is valid"""
    global auth_token, token_expiry
    if not auth_token or datetime.now() > (token_expiry - timedelta(minutes=5)):
        login_to_jenni()

# ==========================================
# STEP 3: Send status update
# ==========================================
def send_status_update(shipment_id, action, details=None):
    ensure_valid_token()
    
    if details is None:
        details = {}
    
    try:
        payload = {
            'system_code': 'YOUR_SYSTEM_CODE',
            'updates': [{
                # ✅ Best practice: use shipment_id (unique) from the push/create response
                'shipment_id': shipment_id,
                'action_code': action,
                'timestamp': datetime.utcnow().isoformat() + 'Z',
                **details
            }]
        }
        
        print(f'📤 Sending {action} update for shipment_id={shipment_id}')
        
        response = requests.post(
            f'{JENNI_API_URL}/v2/push/update-status',
            json=payload,
            headers={
                'Authorization': f'Bearer {auth_token}',
                'Content-Type': 'application/json'
            }
        )
        response.raise_for_status()
        
        print('✅ Status update sent successfully')
        return response.json()
        
    except Exception as error:
        print(f'❌ Failed to send status update: {error}')
        raise

# ==========================================
# USAGE EXAMPLES
# ==========================================
def mark_as_delivered(shipment_id, driver_name):
    """Mark shipment as delivered"""
    return send_status_update(
        shipment_id,
        'SUCCESSFUL_DELIVERY',
        {
            # Optional: proof & GPS (flat fields expected by API)
            'proof_image_url': 'https://your-cdn.com/proof/12345.jpg',
            'received_by_name': 'Ahmed Mohammed',
            'delivery_latitude': 33.312805,
            'delivery_longitude': 44.361488,
            'note': f'Delivered by driver: {driver_name}'
        }
    )

def mark_as_postponed(shipment_id, reason, postponed_date_id):
    """Mark shipment as postponed"""
    return send_status_update(
        shipment_id,
        'POSTPONED',
        {
            'postponed_reason': reason,
            'postponed_reason_en': 'Customer not available',
            'postponed_reason_ku': 'کڕیار بەردەست نییە',
            'postponed_date_id': postponed_date_id,  # ✅ REQUIRED: 1=tomorrow, 2=2 days, 3=3+ days
            'note': 'Will retry delivery tomorrow'
        }
    )

def mark_as_returned(shipment_id, return_reason):
    """Mark shipment as returned"""
    return send_status_update(
        shipment_id,
        'RETURNED_WITH_AGENT',
        {
            'return_reason': return_reason,
            'return_reason_en': 'Customer refused',
            'return_reason_ku': 'کڕیار ڕەتکردەوە',
            'note': 'Customer refused to receive'
        }
    )

# Example usage
if __name__ == '__main__':
    # Successful delivery
    mark_as_delivered(12345, 'Driver Ali')
    
    # Postponed delivery
    mark_as_postponed(12345, 'Customer not available')
    
    # Returned delivery
    mark_as_returned(12345, 'Customer refused')
Step 1: Implement Receive Endpoint (Your Server)
<?php
// receive.php - Receive shipments from Jenni Logistics

header('Content-Type: application/json');

// ==========================================
// STEP 1: Implement /v2/shipments/create endpoint
// ==========================================
if ($_SERVER['REQUEST_METHOD'] === 'POST') {
    error_log('📥 Received shipments from Jenni Logistics');
    
    try {
        // 1. Validate authentication
        $headers = getallheaders();
        $authHeader = $headers['Authorization'] ?? '';
        $token = str_replace('Bearer ', '', $authHeader);
        
        if (empty($token) || !isValidJenniToken($token)) {
            http_response_code(401);
            echo json_encode([
                'success' => false,
                'message' => 'Invalid authentication token'
            ]);
            exit;
        }

        // 2. Extract shipments
        $data = json_decode(file_get_contents('php://input'), true);
        $systemCode = $data['system_code'] ?? '';
        $shipments = $data['shipments'] ?? [];
        
        error_log("System: $systemCode, Shipments: " . count($shipments));

        // 3. Process shipments
        $acceptedShipments = [];
        $rejectedShipments = [];
        
        foreach ($shipments as $shipment) {
            try {
                // Check if shipment already exists in your own system
                if (shipmentExists($shipment['shipment_id'])) {
                    $rejectedShipments[] = [
                        'shipment_number' => $shipment['shipment_number'],
                        'shipment_id' => $shipment['shipment_id'],
                        'airway_bill_number' => $shipment['airway_bill_number'] ?? null,
                        'reason' => 'Shipment already exists in your system',
                        'error_code' => 'DUPLICATE_SHIPMENT',
                        'field' => 'shipment_number'
                    ];
                    continue;
                }
                
                // Save shipment in your own system
                $savedShipment = saveToDatabase($shipment);
                
                // Assign shipment to your delivery driver
                assignToYourDriver($savedShipment);
                
                $acceptedShipments[] = [
                    'shipment_number' => $shipment['shipment_number'],
                    'shipment_id' => $shipment['shipment_id'],
                    'external_id' => (string)$savedShipment['id'],
                    'airway_bill_number' => $shipment['airway_bill_number'] ?? null
                ];
            } catch (Exception $e) {
                $rejectedShipments[] = [
                    'shipment_number' => $shipment['shipment_number'],
                    'shipment_id' => $shipment['shipment_id'] ?? null,
                    'airway_bill_number' => $shipment['airway_bill_number'] ?? null,
                    'reason' => $e->getMessage(),
                    'error_code' => 'PROCESSING_ERROR',
                    'field' => null
                ];
            }
        }

        // 4. Return response (REQUIRED FORMAT)
        echo json_encode([
            'success' => count($acceptedShipments) > 0,
            'message' => count($acceptedShipments) > 0 
                ? count($acceptedShipments) . ' shipment(s) processed successfully'
                : 'All shipments failed to process',
            'timestamp' => gmdate('Y-m-d\TH:i:s\Z'),
            'accepted_shipments' => $acceptedShipments,
            'rejected_shipments' => $rejectedShipments,
            'summary' => [
                'total_requested' => count($shipments),
                'accepted_count' => count($acceptedShipments),
                'rejected_count' => count($rejectedShipments)
            ]
        ]);

    } catch (Exception $error) {
        error_log('❌ Error receiving shipments: ' . $error->getMessage());
        http_response_code(500);
        echo json_encode([
            'success' => false,
            'message' => 'Internal server error',
            'timestamp' => gmdate('Y-m-d\TH:i:s\Z')
        ]);
    }
}

function isValidJenniToken($token) {
    return $token === getenv('JENNI_AUTH_TOKEN');
}

function saveToDatabase($shipment) {
    // Save shipment in your own system
    // Return saved shipment with ID
    return [
        'id' => 12345,
        'shipment_number' => $shipment['shipment_number'],
        'status' => 'PENDING'
    ];
}

function assignToYourDriver($shipment) {
    // Your logic to assign shipment to your delivery driver
}
?>
Step 2: Send Status Updates to Jenni Logistics Server
Important: After receiving shipments from Jenni Logistics, you need to send status updates back to Jenni Logistics server when delivery status changes (e.g., delivered, postponed, returned). 
<?php
// StatusUpdater.php - Send updates to Jenni Logistics server

class JenniStatusUpdater {
    private $apiUrl = 'https://almasara.jenni.delivery/api';  // Jenni Logistics server URL
    private $username;
    private $password;
    private $authToken = null;
    private $tokenExpiry = null;

    public function __construct($username, $password) {
        $this->username = $username;
        $this->password = $password;
    }

    // ==========================================
    // Login to Jenni Logistics server
    // ==========================================
    public function login() {
        $ch = curl_init($this->apiUrl . '/v2/auth/login');
        curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_RETURNTRANSFER, true);
        curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_POST, true);
        curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_HTTPHEADER, ['Content-Type: application/json']);
        curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_POSTFIELDS, json_encode([
            'username' => $this->username,
            'password' => $this->password
        ]));

        $response = curl_exec($ch);
        $httpCode = curl_getinfo($ch, CURLINFO_HTTP_CODE);
        curl_close($ch);

        if ($httpCode !== 200) {
            throw new Exception('Login failed: ' . $response);
        }

        $data = json_decode($response, true);
        $this->authToken = $data['token'];
        $this->tokenExpiry = time() + $data['expires_in'];

        error_log('✅ Logged in to Jenni Logistics successfully');
        return $this->authToken;
    }

    private function ensureValidToken() {
        if (!$this->authToken || time() > ($this->tokenExpiry - 300)) {
            $this->login();
        }
    }

    // ==========================================
    // STEP 3: Send status update
    // ==========================================
    public function sendStatusUpdate($shipmentId, $action, $details = []) {
        $this->ensureValidToken();

        $payload = [
            'system_code' => 'YOUR_SYSTEM_CODE',
            'updates' => [[
                // ✅ Best practice: use shipment_id (unique) from the push/create response
                'shipment_id' => $shipmentId,
                'action_code' => $action,
                'timestamp' => gmdate('Y-m-d\TH:i:s\Z')
            ] + $details]
        ];

        error_log("📤 Sending $action update for shipment_id=$shipmentId");

        $ch = curl_init($this->apiUrl . '/v2/push/update-status');
        curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_RETURNTRANSFER, true);
        curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_POST, true);
        curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_HTTPHEADER, [
            'Content-Type: application/json',
            'Authorization: Bearer ' . $this->authToken
        ]);
        curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_POSTFIELDS, json_encode($payload));

        $response = curl_exec($ch);
        $httpCode = curl_getinfo($ch, CURLINFO_HTTP_CODE);
        curl_close($ch);

        if ($httpCode !== 200) {
            throw new Exception('Failed to send status update: ' . $response);
        }

        error_log('✅ Status update sent successfully');
        return json_decode($response, true);
    }

    // ==========================================
    // USAGE EXAMPLES
    // ==========================================
    public function markAsDelivered($shipmentId, $driverName) {
        return $this->sendStatusUpdate(
            $shipmentId,
            'SUCCESSFUL_DELIVERY',
            [
                // Optional: proof & GPS (flat fields expected by API)
                'proof_image_url' => 'https://your-cdn.com/proof/12345.jpg',
                'received_by_name' => 'Ahmed Mohammed',
                'delivery_latitude' => 33.312805,
                'delivery_longitude' => 44.361488,
                'note' => "Delivered by driver: $driverName"
            ]
        );
    }

    public function markAsPostponed($shipmentId, $reason, $postponedDateId) {
        return $this->sendStatusUpdate(
            $shipmentId,
            'POSTPONED',
            [
                'postponed_reason' => $reason,
                'postponed_reason_en' => 'Customer not available',
                'postponed_reason_ku' => 'کڕیار بەردەست نییە',
                'postponed_date_id' => $postponedDateId,  // ✅ REQUIRED: 1=tomorrow, 2=2 days, 3=3+ days
                'note' => 'Will retry delivery tomorrow'
            ]
        );
    }

    public function markAsReturned($shipmentId, $returnReason) {
        return $this->sendStatusUpdate(
            $shipmentId,
            'RETURNED_WITH_AGENT',
            [
                'return_reason' => $returnReason,
                'return_reason_en' => 'Customer refused',
                'return_reason_ku' => 'کڕیار ڕەتکردەوە',
                'note' => 'Customer refused to receive'
            ]
        );
    }
}

// Example usage
$updater = new JenniStatusUpdater('your_username', 'your_password');

// Successful delivery
$updater->markAsDelivered(12345, 'Driver Ali');

// Postponed delivery
$updater->markAsPostponed(12345, 'Customer not available');

// Returned delivery
$updater->markAsReturned(12345, 'Customer refused');
?>
Step 1: Implement Receive Endpoint (Your Server)
// ShipmentReceiveController.java - Spring Boot Example
@RestController
@RequestMapping("/v2/shipments")
@Slf4j
public class ShipmentReceiveController {

    @Autowired
    private ShipmentService shipmentService;

    // ==========================================
    // STEP 1: Implement /v2/shipments/create endpoint
    // ==========================================
    @PostMapping("/create")
    public ResponseEntity<ReceiveResponse> receiveShipments(
            @RequestHeader("Authorization") String authHeader,
            @RequestBody ReceiveRequest request) {
        
        log.info("📥 Received shipments from Jenni Logistics");
        
        try {
            // 1. Validate authentication
            String token = authHeader.replace("Bearer ", "");
            if (!isValidJenniToken(token)) {
                return ResponseEntity.status(HttpStatus.UNAUTHORIZED)
                    .body(ReceiveResponse.error("Invalid authentication token"));
            }

            // 2. Extract shipments
            String systemCode = request.getSystemCode();
            List<Shipment> shipments = request.getShipments();
            log.info("System: {}, Shipments: {}", systemCode, shipments.size());

            // 3. Process shipments
            List<Map<String, Object>> acceptedShipments = new ArrayList<>();
            List<Map<String, Object>> rejectedShipments = new ArrayList<>();
            
            for (Shipment shipment : shipments) {
                try {
                    // Check if shipment already exists in your own system
                    if (shipmentService.shipmentExists(
                            shipment.getShipmentId())) {
                        Map<String, Object> rejected = new HashMap<>();
                        rejected.put("shipment_number", shipment.getShipmentNumber());
                        rejected.put("shipment_id", shipment.getShipmentId());
                        rejected.put("airway_bill_number", shipment.getAirwayBillNumber());
                        rejected.put("reason", "Shipment number already exists");
                        rejected.put("error_code", "DUPLICATE_SHIPMENT");
                        rejected.put("field", "shipment_number");
                        rejectedShipments.add(rejected);
                        continue;
                    }
                    
                    // Save to database
                    Shipment saved = shipmentService.saveShipment(shipment);
                    
                    // Assign shipment to your delivery driver
                    shipmentService.assignToYourDriver(saved);
                    
                    // Add to accepted
                    Map<String, Object> accepted = new HashMap<>();
                    accepted.put("shipment_number", shipment.getShipmentNumber());
                    accepted.put("shipment_id", shipment.getShipmentId());
                    accepted.put("external_id", String.valueOf(saved.getId()));
                    accepted.put("airway_bill_number", shipment.getAirwayBillNumber());
                    acceptedShipments.add(accepted);
                    
                } catch (Exception e) {
                    Map<String, Object> rejected = new HashMap<>();
                    rejected.put("shipment_number", shipment.getShipmentNumber());
                    rejected.put("shipment_id", shipment.getShipmentId());
                    rejected.put("airway_bill_number", shipment.getAirwayBillNumber());
                    rejected.put("reason", e.getMessage());
                    rejected.put("error_code", "PROCESSING_ERROR");
                    rejected.put("field", null);
                    rejectedShipments.add(rejected);
                }
            }

            // 4. Return response (REQUIRED FORMAT)
            Map<String, Object> response = new HashMap<>();
            response.put("success", !acceptedShipments.isEmpty());
            response.put("message", !acceptedShipments.isEmpty() 
                ? String.format("%d shipment(s) processed successfully", acceptedShipments.size())
                : "All shipments failed to process");
            response.put("timestamp", Instant.now().toString());
            response.put("accepted_shipments", acceptedShipments);
            response.put("rejected_shipments", rejectedShipments);
            response.put("summary", Map.of(
                "total_requested", shipments.size(),
                "accepted_count", acceptedShipments.size(),
                "rejected_count", rejectedShipments.size()
            ));
                
            return ResponseEntity.ok(response);

        } catch (Exception error) {
            log.error("❌ Error receiving shipments", error);
            return ResponseEntity.status(HttpStatus.INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR)
                .body(ReceiveResponse.error("Internal server error"));
        }
    }

    private boolean isValidJenniToken(String token) {
        String expectedToken = System.getenv("JENNI_AUTH_TOKEN");
        return token != null && token.equals(expectedToken);
    }
}
Step 2: Send Status Updates to Jenni Logistics Server
Important: After receiving shipments from Jenni Logistics, you need to send status updates back to Jenni Logistics server when delivery status changes (e.g., delivered, postponed, returned). 
// JenniStatusUpdater.java - Send updates to Jenni Logistics server
@Service
@Slf4j
public class JenniStatusUpdater {

    private static final String JENNI_API_URL = "https://almasara.jenni.delivery/api";
    
    @Value("${jenni.username}")
    private String username;
    
    @Value("${jenni.password}")
    private String password;
    
    private String authToken;
    private Instant tokenExpiry;
    
    private final RestTemplate restTemplate;

    public JenniStatusUpdater(RestTemplate restTemplate) {
        this.restTemplate = restTemplate;
    }

    // ==========================================
    // STEP 2: Login to Jenni Logistics
    // ==========================================
    public void login() {
        try {
            LoginRequest loginRequest = new LoginRequest(username, password);
            
            ResponseEntity<LoginResponse> response = restTemplate.postForEntity(
                JENNI_API_URL + "/v2/auth/login",
                loginRequest,
                LoginResponse.class
            );

            LoginResponse data = response.getBody();
            this.authToken = data.getToken();
            this.tokenExpiry = Instant.now().plusSeconds(data.getExpiresIn());

            log.info("✅ Logged in to Jenni Logistics successfully");
        } catch (Exception e) {
            log.error("❌ Login failed", e);
            throw new RuntimeException("Login failed", e);
        }
    }

    private void ensureValidToken() {
        if (authToken == null || Instant.now().isAfter(tokenExpiry.minusSeconds(300))) {
            login();
        }
    }

    // ==========================================
    // STEP 3: Send status update
    // ==========================================
    public StatusUpdateResponse sendStatusUpdate(
            Long shipmentId,
            String action, 
            Map<String, Object> details) {
        
        ensureValidToken();

        try {
            Map<String, Object> update = new HashMap<>();
            // ✅ Best practice: use shipment_id (unique) from the push/create response
            update.put("shipment_id", shipmentId);
            update.put("action_code", action);
            update.put("timestamp", Instant.now().toString());
            update.putAll(details);

            Map<String, Object> payload = Map.of(
                "system_code", "YOUR_SYSTEM_CODE",
                "updates", List.of(update)
            );

            log.info("📤 Sending {} update for shipment_id={}", action, shipmentId);

            HttpHeaders headers = new HttpHeaders();
            headers.setBearerAuth(authToken);
            headers.setContentType(MediaType.APPLICATION_JSON);

            HttpEntity<Map<String, Object>> request = new HttpEntity<>(payload, headers);

            ResponseEntity<StatusUpdateResponse> response = restTemplate.postForEntity(
                JENNI_API_URL + "/v2/push/update-status",
                request,
                StatusUpdateResponse.class
            );

            log.info("✅ Status update sent successfully");
            return response.getBody();

        } catch (Exception e) {
            log.error("❌ Failed to send status update", e);
            throw new RuntimeException("Failed to send status update", e);
        }
    }

    // ==========================================
    // USAGE EXAMPLES
    // ==========================================
    public StatusUpdateResponse markAsDelivered(Long shipmentId, String driverName) {
        Map<String, Object> details = Map.of(
            // Optional: proof & GPS (flat fields expected by API)
            "proof_image_url", "https://your-cdn.com/proof/12345.jpg",
            "received_by_name", "Ahmed Mohammed",
            "delivery_latitude", 33.312805,
            "delivery_longitude", 44.361488,
            "note", "Delivered by driver: " + driverName
        );
        
        return sendStatusUpdate(shipmentId, "SUCCESSFUL_DELIVERY", details);
    }

    public StatusUpdateResponse markAsPostponed(Long shipmentId, String reason, Integer postponedDateId) {
        Map<String, Object> details = Map.of(
            "postponed_reason", reason,
            "postponed_reason_en", "Customer not available",
            "postponed_reason_ku", "کڕیار بەردەست نییە",
            "postponed_date_id", postponedDateId,  // ✅ REQUIRED: 1=tomorrow, 2=2 days, 3=3+ days
            "note", "Will retry delivery tomorrow"
        );
        
        return sendStatusUpdate(shipmentId, "POSTPONED", details);
    }

    public StatusUpdateResponse markAsReturned(Long shipmentId, String returnReason) {
        Map<String, Object> details = Map.of(
            "return_reason", returnReason,
            "return_reason_en", "Customer refused",
            "return_reason_ku", "کڕیار ڕەتکردەوە",
            "note", "Customer refused to receive"
        );
        
        return sendStatusUpdate(shipmentId, "RETURNED_WITH_AGENT", details);
    }
}

Configuration Required in Jenni Logistics System

Configuration Field Value Description
v2_integration TRUE Enable V2 Push API
receiver_base_url https://your-server.com/v2/shipments/create Your endpoint URL to receive shipments from Jenni Logistics
receiver_auth_mode TOKEN or LOGIN How Jenni Logistics authenticates to your server
receiver_token your_secret_token Static token (if using TOKEN auth)
push_assignment_target Configured Jenni assignment target Which Push Out assignment flow triggers the send

Mode 2: Receive In - Complete Implementation

You create shipments → Send to Jenni Logistics → Jenni Logistics delivers → You track status

Scenario: E-commerce Website

Your Role: Online store that needs Jenni Logistics to deliver customer orders

What You Need to Do:

  1. Create shipments in YOUR system when customers place orders
  2. Send shipments to Jenni Logistics via POST /v2/shipments/create
  3. Implement POST /v2/push/update-status endpoint on YOUR server to receive automatic real-time status updates from Jenni Logistics
  4. (Optional) Use POST /v2/shipments/query for manual status checks anytime

CRITICAL - external_shipment_id (REQUIRED):

  • ✅ You MUST provide external_shipment_id when creating shipments
  • ✅ This is YOUR internal order/shipment ID from your own system
  • ✅ Jenni Logistics saves this ID and uses it when sending status updates back to you
  • ✅ This enables you to match Jenni's status updates with your internal orders
  • ⚠️ If you use your ID as tracking number: Provide the SAME value in both shipment_number AND external_shipment_id fields
  • 📌 Example: If your order ID is 12345 (or any string like "ORD-2024-001", "uuid-123"), send both shipment_number: "12345" AND external_shipment_id: "12345" (both are STRINGS)
  • 💡 Note: external_shipment_id is a STRING field - it can be a number, UUID, alphanumeric code, or any string format. Always send it as a string value.

Automatic Status Updates from Jenni Logistics:

  • ✅ Jenni Logistics automatically sends real-time status updates to YOUR_DOMAIN/v2/push/update-status
  • ✅ Instant notifications when agent delivers, postpones, or returns shipment
  • ✅ Updates include: action_code, current_step, current_stage, agent GPS location, postponed_date_id, return_reason, and more
  • ✅ Updates use your external_shipment_id so you can match them to your orders
  • ✅ See Scenario 8 below for complete implementation with all fields explained
  • JavaScript/Node.js
  • Python
  • PHP
  • Java
Complete Integration Example
// jenni-client.js - Complete Mode 2 Integration
const axios = require('axios');

const JENNI_API_URL = 'https://almasara.jenni.delivery/api';
const JENNI_USERNAME = 'your_username';
const JENNI_PASSWORD = 'your_password';
const SYSTEM_CODE = 'YOUR_SYSTEM_CODE';

class JenniClient {
    constructor() {
        this.authToken = null;
        this.tokenExpiry = null;
    }

    // ==========================================
    // STEP 1: Authentication
    // ==========================================
    async login() {
        try {
            const response = await axios.post(`${JENNI_API_URL}/v2/auth/login`, {
                username: JENNI_USERNAME,
                password: JENNI_PASSWORD
            });

            this.authToken = response.data.token;
            this.tokenExpiry = Date.now() + (response.data.expires_in * 1000);

            console.log('✅ Logged in to Jenni Logistics successfully');
            return this.authToken;
        } catch (error) {
            console.error('❌ Login failed:', error.response?.data || error.message);
            throw error;
        }
    }

    async ensureValidToken() {
        if (!this.authToken || Date.now() > (this.tokenExpiry - 5 * 60 * 1000)) {
            await this.login();
        }
    }

    // ==========================================
    // STEP 2: Create Shipments in Jenni
    // ==========================================
    async createShipments(shipments) {
        await this.ensureValidToken();

        try {
            console.log(`📤 Creating ${shipments.length} shipments in Jenni`);

            const response = await axios.post(
                `${JENNI_API_URL}/v2/shipments/create`,
                {
                    system_code: SYSTEM_CODE,
                    shipments: shipments
                },
                {
                    headers: {
                        'Authorization': `Bearer ${this.authToken}`,
                        'Content-Type': 'application/json',
                        'Accept-Language': 'en'
                    }
                }
            );

            const data = response.data;
            console.log(`✅ Created ${data.summary.accepted_count} shipments`);
            console.log(`❌ Rejected ${data.summary.rejected_count} shipments`);

            // Log rejected shipments
            if (data.rejected_shipments && data.rejected_shipments.length > 0) {
                data.rejected_shipments.forEach(rejected => {
                    console.log(`⚠️ ${rejected.shipment_number}: ${rejected.reason}`);
                });
            }

            return data;
        } catch (error) {
            console.error('❌ Failed to create shipments:', error.response?.data || error.message);
            throw error;
        }
    }

    // ==========================================
    // STEP 3: Query Shipment Status
    // ==========================================
    async queryShipments(shipmentNumbers) {
        await this.ensureValidToken();

        try {
            console.log(`🔍 Querying ${shipmentNumbers.length} shipments`);

            const response = await axios.post(
                `${JENNI_API_URL}/v2/shipments/query`,
                {
                    shipment_numbers: shipmentNumbers
                },
                {
                    headers: {
                        'Authorization': `Bearer ${this.authToken}`,
                        'Content-Type': 'application/json'
                    }
                }
            );

            const data = response.data;
            console.log(`✅ Found ${data.summary.found_count} shipments`);
            console.log(`❌ Not found ${data.summary.not_found_count} shipments`);

            return data;
        } catch (error) {
            console.error('❌ Failed to query shipments:', error.response?.data || error.message);
            throw error;
        }
    }

    // ==========================================
    // STEP 4: Edit Shipment (if needed)
    // ==========================================
    async editShipment(shipmentNumber, updates) {
        await this.ensureValidToken();

        try {
            console.log(`✏️ Editing shipment ${shipmentNumber}`);

            const response = await axios.put(
                `${JENNI_API_URL}/v2/shipments/edit`,
                {
                    shipment_number: shipmentNumber,
                    ...updates
                },
                {
                    headers: {
                        'Authorization': `Bearer ${this.authToken}`,
                        'Content-Type': 'application/json'
                    }
                }
            );

            console.log('✅ Shipment edited successfully');
            return response.data;
        } catch (error) {
            console.error('❌ Failed to edit shipment:', error.response?.data || error.message);
            throw error;
        }
    }

    // ==========================================
    // STEP 5: Update Shipment Status (if needed)
    // ==========================================
    async updateShipmentStatus(shipmentNumber, action, details = {}) {
        await this.ensureValidToken();

        try {
            console.log(`🔄 Updating shipment ${shipmentNumber} to ${action}`);

            const response = await axios.post(
                `${JENNI_API_URL}/v2/shipments/update-status`,
                {
                    shipment_number: shipmentNumber,
                    action: action,
                    ...details
                },
                {
                    headers: {
                        'Authorization': `Bearer ${this.authToken}`,
                        'Content-Type': 'application/json'
                    }
                }
            );

            console.log('✅ Status updated successfully');
            return response.data;
        } catch (error) {
            console.error('❌ Failed to update status:', error.response?.data || error.message);
            throw error;
        }
    }
}

// ==========================================
// USAGE EXAMPLES - E-commerce Integration
// ==========================================

const jenni = new JenniClient();

// Example 1: Customer places order on your website
async function handleNewOrder(order) {
    console.log(`\n📦 New order received: ${order.order_number}`);
    
    try {
        // 1. Create shipment in Jenni
        const shipments = [{
            shipment_number: order.order_number,
            external_shipment_id: order.id.toString(),  // ✅ REQUIRED: Your internal order ID for tracking
            receiver_name: order.customer.name,
            receiver_phone_1: order.customer.phone,
            receiver_phone_2: order.customer.phone2,
            governorate_code: order.delivery_address.governorate_code,
            city: order.delivery_address.city,
            address: order.delivery_address.street,
            amount_iqd: order.total_amount,
            quantity: order.items.length,
            product_info: order.items.map(i => i.name).join(', '),
            note: order.delivery_notes,
            is_proof_of_delivery: true
        }];

        const result = await jenni.createShipments(shipments);

        if (result.summary.accepted_count > 0) {
            // 2. Save Jenni Logistics shipment ID in your own system
            const acceptedShipment = result.accepted_shipments[0];
            await saveShipmentIdToDatabase(
                order.order_number,
                acceptedShipment.shipment_id
            );

            console.log('✅ Order sent to Jenni Logistics for delivery');
            
            // 3. Notify customer
            await sendCustomerNotification(
                order.customer.email,
                'Your order is on its way!',
                `Tracking number: ${acceptedShipment.shipment_id}`
            );
        } else {
            // Handle rejection
            console.error('❌ Order rejected by Jenni');
            await handleOrderRejection(order, result.rejected_shipments[0]);
        }

    } catch (error) {
        console.error('Failed to process order:', error);
        await handleOrderError(order, error);
    }
}

// Example 2: Check shipment status
async function checkOrderStatus(orderNumber) {
    console.log(`\n🔍 Checking status for order: ${orderNumber}`);
    
    try {
        const result = await jenni.queryShipments([orderNumber]);

        if (result.shipments.length > 0) {
            const shipment = result.shipments[0];
            
            console.log(`📊 Status: ${shipment.current_step}`);
            console.log(`📊 Arabic: ${shipment.current_step_ar}`);
            
            // Update status in your own system
            await updateOrderStatusInDatabase(
                orderNumber,
                shipment.current_step,
                shipment.shipment_history
            );

            // Notify customer if delivered
            if (shipment.current_step === 'DELIVERED') {
                await sendCustomerNotification(
                    'customer@email.com',
                    'Your order has been delivered!',
                    'Thank you for your order'
                );
            }

            return shipment;
        } else {
            console.log('❌ Shipment not found');
            return null;
        }
    } catch (error) {
        console.error('Failed to check status:', error);
        throw error;
    }
}

// Example 3: Bulk status check (scheduled job)
async function syncAllOrderStatuses() {
    console.log('\n🔄 Starting bulk status sync...');
    
    try {
        // Get all pending orders from your own system
        const pendingOrders = await getPendingOrdersFromDatabase();
        
        console.log(`Found ${pendingOrders.length} pending orders`);

        // Query in batches of 100 (API limit)
        for (let i = 0; i < pendingOrders.length; i += 100) {
            const batch = pendingOrders.slice(i, i + 100);
            const orderNumbers = batch.map(o => o.order_number);

            const result = await jenni.queryShipments(orderNumbers);

            // Update statuses in your own system
            for (const shipment of result.shipments) {
                await updateOrderStatusInDatabase(
                    shipment.shipment_number,
                    shipment.current_step,
                    shipment.shipment_history
                );
            }

            // Small delay between batches
            await sleep(1000);
        }

        console.log('✅ Bulk status sync completed');
    } catch (error) {
        console.error('Bulk sync failed:', error);
    }
}

// Example 4: Customer wants to change delivery address
async function changeDeliveryAddress(orderNumber, newAddress) {
    console.log(`\n✏️ Changing delivery address for: ${orderNumber}`);
    
    try {
        const result = await jenni.editShipment(orderNumber, {
            governorate_code: newAddress.governorate_code,
            city: newAddress.city,
            address: newAddress.street,
            receiver_phone_1: newAddress.phone
        });

        console.log('✅ Delivery address updated');
        
        // Update in your own system
        await updateAddressInDatabase(orderNumber, newAddress);

        return result;
    } catch (error) {
        console.error('Failed to change address:', error);
        throw error;
    }
}

// Helper functions
async function saveShipmentIdToDatabase(orderNumber, shipmentId) {
    // Your database logic
}

async function updateOrderStatusInDatabase(orderNumber, status, history) {
    // Your database logic
}

async function sendCustomerNotification(email, subject, message) {
    // Your email/SMS logic
}

async function getPendingOrdersFromDatabase() {
    // Your database logic
    return [];
}

function sleep(ms) {
    return new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, ms));
}

// ==========================================
// Run Examples
// ==========================================

// Test: Create new order
const sampleOrder = {
    id: 12345,  // ✅ Your internal order ID (will be sent as external_shipment_id)
    order_number: 'ORD-2024-001',
    customer: {
        name: 'Ahmed Mohammed',
        phone: '07901234567',
        phone2: '07801234567',
        email: 'ahmed@email.com'
    },

    },

        { name: 'Mobile Phone', quantity: 1 },
        { name: 'Phone Case', quantity: 1 }

// handleNewOrder(sampleOrder);

module.exports = { JenniClient, handleNewOrder, checkOrderStatus, syncAllOrderStatuses };
Complete Integration Example
# jenni_client.py - Complete Mode 2 Integration
import requests
from datetime import datetime, timedelta
from typing import List, Dict, Optional

JENNI_API_URL = 'https://almasara.jenni.delivery/api'
JENNI_USERNAME = 'your_username'
JENNI_PASSWORD = 'your_password'
SYSTEM_CODE = 'YOUR_SYSTEM_CODE'

class JenniClient:
    def __init__(self):
        self.auth_token = None
        self.token_expiry = None

    # ==========================================
    # STEP 1: Authentication
    # ==========================================
    def login(self):
        """Login to Jenni Logistics and get auth token"""
        try:
            response = requests.post(
                f'{JENNI_API_URL}/v2/auth/login',
                json={
                    'username': JENNI_USERNAME,
                    'password': JENNI_PASSWORD
                }
            )
            response.raise_for_status()
            data = response.json()

            self.auth_token = data['token']
            self.token_expiry = datetime.now() + timedelta(seconds=data['expires_in'])

            print('✅ Logged in to Jenni Logistics successfully')
            return self.auth_token
        except Exception as error:
            print(f'❌ Login failed: {error}')
            raise

    def ensure_valid_token(self):
        """Ensure auth token is valid"""
        if not self.auth_token or datetime.now() > (self.token_expiry - timedelta(minutes=5)):
            self.login()

    # ==========================================
    # STEP 2: Create Shipments
    # ==========================================
    def create_shipments(self, shipments: List[Dict]) -> Dict:
        """Create shipments in Jenni"""
        self.ensure_valid_token()

        try:
            print(f'📤 Creating {len(shipments)} shipments in Jenni')

            response = requests.post(
                f'{JENNI_API_URL}/v2/shipments/create',
                json={
                    'system_code': SYSTEM_CODE,
                    'shipments': shipments
                },
                headers={
                    'Authorization': f'Bearer {self.auth_token}',
                    'Content-Type': 'application/json',
                    'Accept-Language': 'en'
                }
            )
            response.raise_for_status()
            data = response.json()

            print(f'✅ Created {data["summary"]["accepted_count"]} shipments')
            print(f'❌ Rejected {data["summary"]["rejected_count"]} shipments')

            # Log rejected shipments
            if data.get('rejected_shipments'):
                for rejected in data['rejected_shipments']:
                    print(f'⚠️ {rejected["shipment_number"]}: {rejected["reason"]}')

            return data
        except Exception as error:
            print(f'❌ Failed to create shipments: {error}')
            raise

    # ==========================================
    # STEP 3: Query Shipments
    # ==========================================
    def query_shipments(self, shipment_numbers: List[str]) -> Dict:
        """Query shipment status"""
        self.ensure_valid_token()

        try:
            print(f'🔍 Querying {len(shipment_numbers)} shipments')

            response = requests.post(
                f'{JENNI_API_URL}/v2/shipments/query',
                json={'shipment_numbers': shipment_numbers},
                headers={
                    'Authorization': f'Bearer {self.auth_token}',
                    'Content-Type': 'application/json'
                }
            )
            response.raise_for_status()
            data = response.json()

            print(f'✅ Found {data["summary"]["found_count"]} shipments')
            print(f'❌ Not found {data["summary"]["not_found_count"]} shipments')

            return data
        except Exception as error:
            print(f'❌ Failed to query shipments: {error}')
            raise

    # ==========================================
    # STEP 4: Edit Shipment
    # ==========================================
    def edit_shipment(self, shipment_number: str, updates: Dict) -> Dict:
        """Edit shipment information"""
        self.ensure_valid_token()

        try:
            print(f'✏️ Editing shipment {shipment_number}')

            response = requests.put(
                f'{JENNI_API_URL}/v2/shipments/edit',
                json={
                    'shipment_number': shipment_number,
                    **updates
                },
                headers={
                    'Authorization': f'Bearer {self.auth_token}',
                    'Content-Type': 'application/json'
                }
            )
            response.raise_for_status()

            print('✅ Shipment edited successfully')
            return response.json()
        except Exception as error:
            print(f'❌ Failed to edit shipment: {error}')
            raise


# ==========================================
# USAGE EXAMPLES - E-commerce Integration
# ==========================================

jenni = JenniClient()

def handle_new_order(order: Dict):
    """Handle new customer order"""
    print(f'\n📦 New order received: {order["order_number"]}')

    try:
        # 1. Create shipment in Jenni
        shipments = [{
            'shipment_number': order['order_number'],
            'external_shipment_id': str(order['id']),  # ✅ REQUIRED: Your internal order ID for tracking
            'receiver_name': order['customer']['name'],
            'receiver_phone_1': order['customer']['phone'],
            'governorate_code': order['delivery_address']['governorate_code'],
            'city': order['delivery_address']['city'],
            'address': order['delivery_address']['street'],
            'amount_iqd': order['total_amount'],
            'quantity': len(order['items']),
            'product_info': ', '.join(item['name'] for item in order['items']),
            'note': order.get('delivery_notes', ''),
            'is_proof_of_delivery': True
        }]

        result = jenni.create_shipments(shipments)

        if result['summary']['accepted_count'] > 0:
            # 2. Save Jenni Logistics shipment ID
            accepted = result['accepted_shipments'][0]
            save_shipment_id_to_database(
                order['order_number'],
                accepted['shipment_id']
            )

            print('✅ Order sent to Jenni Logistics for delivery')

            # 3. Notify customer
            send_customer_notification(
                order['customer']['email'],
                'Your order is on its way!',
                f'Tracking number: {accepted["shipment_id"]}'
            )
        else:
            print('❌ Order rejected by Jenni')

    except Exception as error:
        print(f'Failed to process order: {error}')


def check_order_status(order_number: str):
    """Check order status"""
    print(f'\n🔍 Checking status for order: {order_number}')

    try:
        result = jenni.query_shipments([order_number])

        if result['shipments']:
            shipment = result['shipments'][0]

            print(f'📊 Status: {shipment["current_step"]}')
            print(f'📊 Arabic: {shipment["current_step_ar"]}')

            # Update your own system
            update_order_status_in_database(
                order_number,
                shipment['current_step'],
                shipment['shipment_history']
            )

            # Notify if delivered
            if shipment['current_step'] == 'DELIVERED':
                send_customer_notification(
                    'customer@email.com',
                    'Your order has been delivered!',
                    'Thank you for your order'
                )

            return shipment
        else:
            print('❌ Shipment not found')
            return None

    except Exception as error:
        print(f'Failed to check status: {error}')
        raise


# Helper functions (implement these based on your system)
def save_shipment_id_to_database(order_number, shipment_id):
    pass

def update_order_status_in_database(order_number, status, history):
    pass

def send_customer_notification(email, subject, message):
    pass


# Test example
if __name__ == '__main__':
    sample_order = {
        'id': 12345,  # ✅ Your internal order ID (will be sent as external_shipment_id)
        'order_number': 'ORD-2024-001',
        'customer': {
            'name': 'Ahmed Mohammed',
            'phone': '07901234567',
            'email': 'ahmed@email.com'
        },
        'delivery_address': {
            'governorate_code': 'BGD',
            'city': 'Al-Karrada',
            'street': 'Al-Rabi Street, Building 5'
        },
        'total_amount': 50000,
        'items': [
            {'name': 'Mobile Phone', 'quantity': 1},
            {'name': 'Phone Case', 'quantity': 1}
        ],
        'delivery_notes': 'Please call before delivery'
    }

    # handle_new_order(sample_order)
    # check_order_status('ORD-2024-001')
PHP example follows the same structure as JavaScript - implement authentication, create shipments, query status, and handle webhooks.
Java/Spring Boot example follows the same structure - use RestTemplate or WebClient for API calls.

Configuration Required in Jenni Logistics System

Configuration Field Value Description
system_code YOUR_UNIQUE_CODE Your system identifier in Jenni Logistics (e.g., ECOMMERCE_STORE_01)
username your_username Your login username for Jenni Logistics API
password your_secure_password Your login password for Jenni Logistics API
webhook_base_url https://your-site.com (Optional) Your domain - Jenni Logistics will automatically send status updates to YOUR_DOMAIN/v2/push/update-status
receiving_assignment_target Configured Jenni receiving target The Jenni assignment flow that receives your shipments

Integration Workflow

Complete Integration Flow

Start by choosing your integration path. The direct shipment APIs and the integration/webhook APIs are both valid, but they are used in different directions.

Path 1: Receive In / Direct Jenni APIs

Use this path if you create shipments in your own system and want Jenni to deliver them.

  • You call /v2/auth/login, /v2/shipments/create, and optionally /v2/shipments/query
  • You may also use /v2/shipments/update-status as a direct shipment-management API when needed
  • If you want automatic updates from Jenni, implement YOUR_DOMAIN/v2/push/update-status
Path 2: Push Out / Integration Flow

Use this path if Jenni sends shipments to you and your team delivers them.

  • Implement YOUR_DOMAIN/v2/shipments/create to receive shipments from Jenni
  • Send your delivery results back to Jenni using /v2/push/update-status
  • For full synchronization, also implement YOUR_DOMAIN/v2/push/update-status for optional Jenni follow-up callbacks
Quick rule: /v2/shipments/* = direct shipment-management APIs on Jenni. /v2/push/update-status = integration/webhook-style status exchange between Jenni and external systems.
Detailed Direct API Workflow

The detailed steps below describe the common Receive In / direct API path where your system calls Jenni directly.

1
Registration & Setup
  • Contact support to register your company
  • Confirm your chosen system_code (company identifier)
  • Get your login credentials (username and password)
  • Determine your role: MERCHANT or AGGREGATOR
📧 Contact: thulfiqar.a@wedatum.co to get started
↓
2
Authentication
POST https://almasara.jenni.delivery/api/v2/auth/login
{
  "username": "your_username",
  "password": "your_password"
}

Response:
{
  "token": "eyJhbGciOi...",
  "refreshToken": "eyJhbGciOi..."
}
⏱️ Token Validity: Access token = 24 hours, Refresh token = 30 days
↓
3
Download Reference Data (One-time)

Download and cache these lists locally for validation:

  • GET /v2/reference/governorates?download=excel
  • GET /v2/reference/cities?download=excel
  • GET /v2/reference/return-reasons?download=excel
  • GET /v2/reference/postponed-reasons?download=excel
💡 Tip: Cache these lists locally and refresh weekly to stay up-to-date
↓
4
Create Shipments

This step creates shipments directly on Jenni using the shipment APIs.

POST https://almasara.jenni.delivery/api/v2/shipments/create
Headers: Authorization: Bearer {token}
{
  "system_code": "YOUR_CODE",
  "shipments": [
    {
      "shipment_number": "SHIP001",
      "receiver_name": "Ahmed Ali",
      "receiver_phone_1": "07901234567",
      "governorate_code": "BGD",
      "city": "Al-Karrada",
      "amount_iqd": 50000
      // ... other fields
    }
  ]
}
✅ Result: You receive shipment_id for each accepted shipment
⚠️ Important: Store the shipment_id in your own system - you'll need it when updating shipment status or tracking shipments.
↓
5
Query & Track Shipments
POST https://almasara.jenni.delivery/api/v2/shipments/query
Headers: Authorization: Bearer {token}
{
  "shipment_numbers": ["SHIP001", "SHIP002"]
}

Response includes:
- Current status (current_step)
- Delivery agent info
- Settlement info
- Full history
↓
6
Direct Shipment Update (Optional)
Important: This is the direct endpoint /v2/shipments/update-status, not the integration/webhook endpoint /v2/push/update-status.

Step 6a: Check available actions first

GET https://almasara.jenni.delivery/api/v2/reference/available-actions?shipment_number=SHIP001
Headers: Authorization: Bearer {token}

Step 6b: Perform the action

POST https://almasara.jenni.delivery/api/v2/shipments/update-status
Headers: Authorization: Bearer {token}
{
  "shipment_id": 12345,
  "action": "POSTPONED",
  "postponed_reason": "العميل غير موجود",
  "postponed_reason_en": "Customer not available",
  "postponed_reason_ku": "کڕیار بەردەست نییە",
  "postponed_date_id": 1
}
↓
7
Monitor & Statistics
# Order counts by status
GET https://almasara.jenni.delivery/api/v2/statistics/orders-by-status
# Orders in process
GET https://almasara.jenni.delivery/api/v2/orders/in-process
Push Out Workflow in 3 Steps
  1. Jenni → Your server: implement YOUR_DOMAIN/v2/shipments/create to receive shipments.
  2. You → Jenni: send delivery updates to https://almasara.jenni.delivery/api/v2/push/update-status.
  3. Optional Jenni → Your server: implement YOUR_DOMAIN/v2/push/update-status if you want later Jenni follow-up callbacks such as TREATED, POSTPONEMENT_APPROVED, or RETURN_APPROVED.
✨ Integration Best Practices
Recommended Practices
  • Cache reference data: Download governorates/cities once, update weekly
  • Token refresh: Implement automatic refresh before expiration
  • Batch operations: Group shipments (up to 100) for efficiency
  • Error handling: Parse error_code and field to show user-friendly messages
  • Available actions: Always check before allowing user to perform action
  • Validate locally: Check phone format, amounts, etc. before API call
Performance Tips
  • Use bulk APIs: Create/query multiple shipments in one request
  • Implement retry logic: For network failures (with exponential backoff)
  • Handle timeouts: Set reasonable timeout (30 seconds recommended)
  • Log API calls: Keep request/response logs for debugging
  • Monitor rate limits: Don't exceed recommended request rates
✅ Integration Checklist
Before Going Live:
  • ☐ Test login with your credentials
  • ☐ Successfully create test shipments
  • ☐ Query shipments and parse response
  • ☐ Test update status operations
  • ☐ Download all reference data
  • ☐ Implement token refresh logic
Production Ready:
  • ☐ Error handling implemented
  • ☐ Validation rules applied
  • ☐ Logging configured
  • ☐ Retry mechanism in place
  • ☐ Reference data cached
  • ☐ Performance tested

Key Features

Secure Authentication

JWT token-based authentication with 24h access + 30-day refresh tokens

Unified Action Codes

Professional, standardized action codes (SUCCESSFUL_DELIVERY, RETURN_TO_STORE, etc.)

Global Standards

26 step statuses + 40+ action codes mapped to international conventions

Multilingual Support

Full Arabic + English responses with RTL/LTR support

Bulk Operations

Create up to 100 shipments or query up to 100 in a single API call. Pagination supports 100 per page.

Field-Level Validation

Detailed validation with specific error codes and field-level rejection reasons

Geographic Validation

Real-time validation for 18 governorates and 100+ cities across Iraq

Multi-Currency Support

Accept payments in IQD and USD with automatic conversion tracking

Reference Data APIs

Access governorates, cities, reasons, and action codes (some public, some protected)

Flexible Downloads

Export reference data in Excel or JSON formats for offline use

Partial Returns

Support for partial delivery/returns with quantity and amount tracking

Interactive Documentation

Live API tester, 5 language examples (cURL, JS, Python, Java, PHP)

Bidirectional Integration

Full V2 support for any two systems with external_shipment_id tracking and status synchronization

Dual Retry Mechanism

Immediate retry (1s-4s) + Persistent retry (15min-60min) for all V2 integrations

Smart Shipment Lookup

Priority lookup for all V2 systems using shipment_id, then external_id, then shipment_number with ownership/context validation.

Sender Information

Track sender_name and sender_phone from any V2 source system for complete origin details

More Features
  • ✅ Real-time status tracking
  • ✅ Postponement up to 3 days
  • ✅ Image upload for proof of delivery
  • ✅ Pagination for large datasets (100/page)
  • ✅ Bidirectional V2 integration support
  • ✅ Comprehensive audit logging
  • ✅ Agent assignment & tracking
  • ✅ Amount change handling
  • ✅ Duplicate detection (batch optimized)
  • ✅ Persistent retry (24h window)
  • ✅ Statistics & reporting APIs
  • ✅ Delete/recall shipments
  • ✅ Edit shipment details
  • ✅ Generate shipping stickers
  • ✅ V1 & V2 compatibility

Push & Status Update APIs (Push Out Mode)

Understanding Push Out Mode

Push Out Mode means Jenni Logistics pushes shipments to your delivery system. This starts as two-way communication and may later become bidirectional again if Jenni sends follow-up callbacks for the same shipment:

  1. Jenni → Your Server: Jenni Logistics automatically sends new shipments to your endpoint
  2. Your Server → Jenni: You send status updates back to Jenni Logistics when delivery status changes
  3. Jenni → Your Server (optional follow-up): Jenni may later send callback updates such as TREATED, POSTPONEMENT_APPROVED, or RETURN_APPROVED
Two Different Servers

Important: The two APIs below run on different servers:

Step 1: Jenni Logistics → Your Server
  • What: Jenni Logistics sends shipments to you
  • Endpoint: YOUR_DOMAIN/v2/shipments/create
  • You must: Implement this endpoint on YOUR server
Step 2: Your Server → Jenni
  • What: You send status updates back to Jenni Logistics
  • Endpoint: JENNI_DOMAIN/v2/push/update-status
  • You must: Call Jenni's endpoint when status changes
Optional Step 3: Jenni Logistics → Your Server Again
  • What: Jenni may send follow-up callbacks for a shipment already pushed to you
  • Endpoint: YOUR_DOMAIN/v2/push/update-status
  • Typical actions: TREATED, POSTPONEMENT_APPROVED, RETURN_APPROVED
  • Recommendation: Implement this endpoint too if you want full synchronization with Jenni after the first push
Automatic Trigger: When shipments enter your configured Push Out assignment flow, Jenni Logistics automatically sends them to your receiver URL in real-time. No manual triggering required!
Bidirectional Follow-up Note

Push Out can become bidirectional after the initial shipment handoff. If a shipment that was already pushed to your system is later handled inside Jenni (for example TREATED, POSTPONEMENT_APPROVED, or RETURN_APPROVED), Jenni can notify your receiver again using the same V2 webhook-style payload documented for YOUR_DOMAIN/v2/push/update-status.

For TREATED, expect a dedicated treated_message field in addition to the general note.

Practical Rule for Beneficiaries

If you use Push Out and you only care about receiving the shipment and sending your own delivery result, the minimum implementation is:

  • POST YOUR_DOMAIN/v2/shipments/create
  • Call POST JENNI_DOMAIN/v2/push/update-status

If you also want Jenni to notify you later when Jenni internally handles the same shipment, implement POST YOUR_DOMAIN/v2/push/update-status as well.

POST /v2/shipments/create On YOUR Server Step 1: Jenni Logistics → You

Purpose: Jenni Logistics automatically sends shipments to this endpoint on YOUR server when they are ready for your delivery workflow.

Important: You must implement this endpoint on YOUR server, not on Jenni Logistics server. Jenni Logistics will call this endpoint to push shipments to your system.
Configuration:
  • In Jenni Logistics integration settings, configure your Push Out receiver base URL to your domain (e.g., https://yourstore.com or https://yourstore.com/api)
  • Jenni automatically appends /v2/shipments/create to your configured URL
  • Final endpoint: YOUR_DOMAIN/v2/shipments/create
  • ⚠️ You MUST implement this exact path on your server
Request Headers (Jenni Sends to Your Server):
POST YOUR_DOMAIN/v2/shipments/create
Content-Type: application/json
Authorization: Bearer {token}  
// Token is either a configured static bearer token
// or a dynamic token obtained through your LOGIN authentication flow
Request Body:
{
  "system_code": "YOUR_SYSTEM_CODE",
  "shipments": [
    {
      "shipment_id": 12345,  // Unique shipment ID from the sending system - use for matching
      "shipment_number": "SHIP-001",  // Receipt number (can be duplicate!)
      "external_shipment_id": "12345",  // Optional external reference if your flow uses one
      "receiver_name": "أحمد علي",
      "receiver_phone_1": "07901234567",
      "governorate_code": "BGD",  // Global code
      "city_name": "الكرادة",     // Preferred city field
      "city": "الكرادة",          // Optional compatibility alias if your receiver expects it
      "address": "شارع 14، بناية 5",
      "amount_iqd": 50000,
      "amount_usd": 0,
      "quantity": 2,
      "note": "Handle with care",
      "sender_name": "متجر الإلكترونيات",
      "sender_phone": "07701234567",
      "is_proof_of_delivery": false,
      "is_fragile": false
    }
  ]
}
CRITICAL: shipment_id vs shipment_number
  • shipment_id: Unique case identifier - ALWAYS unique across entire system
  • shipment_number: Receipt/tracking number - CAN BE DUPLICATED across different customers
  • ⚠️ NEVER use shipment_number alone for matching - it can be duplicate!
  • ✅ ALWAYS use shipment_id for safe matching, or shipment_number + customer_id combination
Compatibility Notes

This payload includes common V2 fields used by receiving systems:

  • ✅ shipment_id for reliable matching
  • ✅ shipment_number as a human-readable tracking reference
  • ✅ city_name as the preferred city field, with optional city alias when needed
  • ✅ external_shipment_id only if your integration flow uses an external reference
CRITICAL: Response Format Requirements

Your endpoint MUST return the following format EXACTLY:

  • ✅ accepted_shipments array (not "results")
  • ✅ rejected_shipments array (for failed shipments)
  • ✅ summary object with total_requested, accepted_count, rejected_count
  • ✅ success boolean, message string, timestamp string
  • ❌ DO NOT use "results" array - this format is deprecated
Expected Response (REQUIRED FORMAT):
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "1 shipment processed successfully",
  "timestamp": "2025-11-03T05:15:51Z",
  "accepted_shipments": [
    {
      "shipment_number": "SHIP-001",
      "shipment_id": 12345,
      "external_id": "EXT-12345",  // Your internal shipment ID (required for sync back to Jenni)
      "airway_bill_number": "AWB-789"  // Optional
    }
  ],
  "rejected_shipments": [
    {
      "shipment_number": "94777598",
      "shipment_id": null,
      "airway_bill_number": null,
      "reason": "Shipment number already exists",
      "error_code": "DUPLICATE_SHIPMENT",
      "field": "shipment_number"
    }
  ],
  "summary": {
    "total_requested": 2,
    "accepted_count": 1,
    "rejected_count": 1
  }
}
Response Fields Explained:
Field Type Description
success Boolean true if at least one shipment was accepted, false if all rejected
message String Human-readable status message (e.g., "1 shipment processed successfully")
timestamp String ISO 8601 timestamp (e.g., "2025-11-03T05:15:51Z")
accepted_shipments Array REQUIRED: List of successfully processed shipments. Each item should include shipment_number and external_id (your internal shipment/order ID returned back to Jenni for sync). shipment_id may also be returned for convenience if your system keeps the same inbound ID.
rejected_shipments Array REQUIRED: List of rejected shipments. Each item must include shipment_number, error_code, reason, and field (field that caused error)
summary Object REQUIRED: Summary statistics with total_requested, accepted_count, rejected_count
Implementation Example (Your Server):
@RestController
@RequestMapping("/v2")
public class JenniIntegrationController {
    
    @PostMapping("/shipments/create")
    public ResponseEntity<Map<String, Object>> receiveShipments(
            @RequestBody Map<String, Object> request,
            @RequestHeader("Authorization") String authHeader) {
        
        // 1. Validate authentication token
        if (!isValidToken(authHeader)) {
            return ResponseEntity.status(401).body(Map.of("error", "Invalid token"));
        }
        
        // 2. Extract shipments from request
        List<Map<String, Object>> shipments = (List) request.get("shipments");
        List<Map<String, Object>> acceptedShipments = new ArrayList<>();
        List<Map<String, Object>> rejectedShipments = new ArrayList<>();
        int successCount = 0;
        int failedCount = 0;
        
        for (Map<String, Object> shipment : shipments) {
            try {
                // ✅ CRITICAL: Use shipment_id for safe matching
                Long shipmentId = Long.valueOf(shipment.get("shipment_id").toString());
                String receiptNo = (String) shipment.get("shipment_number");
                
                // Check if shipment already exists in your own system
                if (shipmentExistsInDatabase(shipmentId, receiptNo)) {
                    rejectedShipments.add(Map.of(
                        "shipment_number", receiptNo,
                        "shipment_id", shipmentId,
                        "airway_bill_number", shipment.get("airway_bill_number") != null 
                            ? shipment.get("airway_bill_number").toString() : null,
                        "reason", "Shipment already exists in your system",
                        "error_code", "DUPLICATE_SHIPMENT",
                        "field", "shipment_number"
                    ));
                    failedCount++;
                    continue;
                }
                
                // Save shipment in your own system
                String externalId = saveToDatabase(shipment);
                
                // Add to accepted shipments
                Map<String, Object> accepted = new HashMap<>();
                accepted.put("shipment_number", receiptNo);
                accepted.put("shipment_id", shipmentId);
                accepted.put("external_id", externalId);  // Your internal ID
                accepted.put("airway_bill_number", shipment.get("airway_bill_number"));
                acceptedShipments.add(accepted);
                successCount++;
                
            } catch (Exception e) {
                Long shipmentId = shipment.get("shipment_id") != null 
                    ? Long.valueOf(shipment.get("shipment_id").toString()) : null;
                
                rejectedShipments.add(Map.of(
                    "shipment_number", shipment.get("shipment_number").toString(),
                    "shipment_id", shipmentId,
                    "airway_bill_number", shipment.get("airway_bill_number") != null 
                        ? shipment.get("airway_bill_number").toString() : null,
                    "reason", e.getMessage(),
                    "error_code", "PROCESSING_ERROR",
                    "field", null
                ));
                failedCount++;
            }
        }
        
        // Build response in REQUIRED format
        Map<String, Object> response = new HashMap<>();
        response.put("success", successCount > 0);
        response.put("message", successCount > 0 
            ? String.format("%d shipment(s) processed successfully", successCount)
            : "All shipments failed to process");
        response.put("timestamp", Instant.now().toString());
        response.put("accepted_shipments", acceptedShipments);
        response.put("rejected_shipments", rejectedShipments);
        response.put("summary", Map.of(
            "total_requested", shipments.size(),
            "accepted_count", successCount,
            "rejected_count", failedCount
        ));
        
        return ResponseEntity.ok(response);
    }
}
POST /v2/push/update-status On Jenni Logistics Server Step 2: You → Jenni

Purpose: Your system calls this endpoint on Jenni Logistics server to send status updates back when delivery status changes.

Important: This endpoint exists on Jenni server. You call this endpoint from YOUR server to send status updates to Jenni Logistics (e.g., when driver delivers, postpones, or returns shipment).
Request (You Send to Jenni Logistics):
{
  "system_code": "YOUR_SYSTEM_CODE",
  "updates": [
    {
      "shipment_id": 12345,  // ✅ BEST: Use shipment_id from original push
      "shipment_number": "SHIP-001",  // Optional: receipt number
      "action_code": "SUCCESSFUL_DELIVERY",  // Global action code
      "timestamp": "2025-10-31T15:30:00Z",
      "note": "Delivered successfully"
      
      // === POSTPONED Action ===
      // ✅ REQUIRED fields for POSTPONED action:
      "postponed_reason": "العميل غير موجود",  // Required for POSTPONED
      "postponed_reason_en": "Customer not available",  // Optional: English description
      "postponed_reason_ku": "کڕیار بەردەست نییە",  // Optional: Kurdish description
      "postponed_date_id": 1,  // Required: 1=tomorrow, 2=in 2 days, 3=3+ days
      
      // === RETURN Actions (RETURNED_WITH_AGENT, RETURN_TO_STORE) ===
      // ✅ REQUIRED fields for return actions:
      "return_reason": "رفض الاستلام",  // Required for any RETURN action
      "return_reason_en": "Customer refused",  // Optional: English description
      "return_reason_ku": "کڕیار ڕەتکردەوە",  // Optional: Kurdish description
      
      // === TREATED ===
      // ✅ REQUIRED for action_code="TREATED"
      "treated_message": "تم التواصل مع العميل - موعد جديد غداً",
      
      // === OTHER Optional Fields ===
      "new_amount_iqd": 45000,  // For AMOUNT_CHANGE actions
      "proof_image_url": "https://cdn.example.com/proof.jpg",
      "delivery_latitude": 33.3152,
      "delivery_longitude": 44.3661,
      
      // === Partial Delivery ===
      "is_partial": false,
      "quantity_delivered": 2,
      "quantity_returned": 0,
      "partial_has_returned": true,
      "partial_return_action": "RETURN_TO_STORE"
    }
  ]
}
Authentication: Send your Jenni JWT token in the header: Authorization: Bearer {token}. Do not send authentication fields inside the JSON body.
Required Fields by action_code (Backend-Validated)

Always send shipment_id + action_code. Extra required fields depend on action_code:

action_code Required fields Notes
POSTPONED postponed_reason, postponed_date_id postponed_date_id must be 1..3
RETURNED_WITH_AGENT / RETURN_TO_STORE return_reason Free text is accepted
TREATED treated_message Min length 3 chars
POSTPONEMENT_APPROVED postponed_reason, postponed_date_id Jenni keeps the postponement details in the callback
RETURN_APPROVED return_reason Jenni keeps the return reason in the callback
SUCCESSFUL_DELIVERY_WITH_AMOUNT_CHANGE new_amount_iqd or new_amount_usd At least one must be > 0
All other action codes No extra required fields You may still send optional fields like proof/GPS
Implementation Example (You Call Jenni):
// When your driver updates delivery status
async function sendStatusToJenni(shipmentId, actionCode) {
    const request = {
        system_code: "YOUR_SYSTEM_CODE",
        updates: [{
            shipment_id: shipmentId,  // ✅ Use the ID we received from Jenni Logistics
            action_code: actionCode,   // e.g., "SUCCESSFUL_DELIVERY"
            timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
            note: "Status updated by driver"
        }]
    };
    
    const response = await fetch('https://almasara.jenni.delivery/api/v2/push/update-status', {
        method: 'POST',
        headers: {
            'Content-Type': 'application/json',
            'Authorization': `Bearer ${YOUR_JENNI_TOKEN}`
        },
        body: JSON.stringify(request)
    });
    
    const result = await response.json();
    console.log('Status update result:', result);
}

Global Action Codes

Use these standardized codes when sending status updates:

Action Code Arabic / العربية When to Use Required Fields
SUCCESSFUL_DELIVERY سُلمت بنجاح Shipment delivered successfully to customer None
POSTPONED مؤجل Delivery postponed to another date postponed_reason
postponed_date_id 1=tomorrow, 2=2 days, 3=3+ days
RETURNED_WITH_AGENT راجع عند المندوب Shipment returned, still with delivery agent return_reason
RETURN_TO_STORE راجع للمخزن Shipment returned to warehouse/store return_reason
TREATED تم معالجة الطلب Call center / follow-up handled (treated) treated_message Required by backend validation
POSTPONEMENT_APPROVED تأكيد التأجيل Jenni confirmed the postponement after follow-up postponed_reason
postponed_date_id
RETURN_APPROVED تأكيد الراجع Jenni confirmed the shipment return after review return_reason
PARTIAL_DELIVERY تسليم جزئي Some items delivered, some returned is_partial=true
quantity_delivered
quantity_returned
partial_return_action
SUCCESSFUL_DELIVERY_WITH_AMOUNT_CHANGE سُلمت بتغيير مبلغ Delivered with different amount (negotiated) new_amount_iqd / new_amount_usd
CUSTOMER_UNREACHABLE العميل غير متاح Cannot contact customer None
WRONG_ADDRESS عنوان خاطئ Address is incorrect or incomplete None

Authentication Methods

Method 1: Static TOKEN

Best for: Simple integrations with fixed token

Configuration:

Admin Configuration Required: Contact your system administrator to configure static token authentication for your integration.

How it Works:
  • Jenni sends the configured bearer token with each request
  • Sends as Authorization: Bearer {token}
  • No expiration - token never changes
  • Simple but less secure
Method 2: Dynamic LOGIN

Best for: Secure integrations with token refresh

Configuration:

Admin Configuration Required: Contact your system administrator to configure LOGIN authentication with username/password for your integration.

How it Works:
  • Jenni calls your login endpoint first
  • Gets JWT token with expiration
  • Caches token and auto-refreshes
  • More secure, supports rotation

Best Practices & Security

DO:
  • ✅ Always use shipment_id for matching - it's unique
  • ✅ Return shipment_id in response, not receipt number
  • ✅ Validate authentication on every request
  • ✅ Log all incoming shipments with timestamps
  • ✅ Handle duplicate shipments gracefully (idempotency)
  • ✅ Send status updates immediately when status changes
  • ✅ Use global action codes (SUCCESSFUL_DELIVERY, etc.)
  • ✅ Include proof of delivery images when available
DON'T:
  • ❌ Never use receipt number alone for matching
  • ❌ Never rely on shipment_number alone without your own ownership/context check
  • ❌ Don't modify shipment_id or shipment_number
  • ❌ Don't store tokens in plain text
  • ❌ Don't skip validation - check every field
  • ❌ Don't send status updates in batches - send immediately
  • ❌ Don't use custom action codes - use global standards
  • ❌ Don't ignore partial delivery scenarios

Retry Mechanism & Reliability

Built-in Reliability for All V2 Systems

V2 Integration API includes an automatic retry mechanism with exponential backoff to ensure reliable delivery of shipments and status updates for ALL V2-compatible systems (Jenni, external systems, or any V2 integration) even when network issues occur.

How Automatic Retry Works

When ANY V2 system sends requests (shipments or status updates), it automatically retries failed requests:

Retryable Errors

V2 systems will automatically retry these errors:

  • ✅ Network errors (connection refused, timeout, reset)
  • ✅ Server errors (500, 502, 503, 504)
  • ✅ Rate limiting (429 Too Many Requests)
  • ✅ Request timeout (408)
Result: Temporary issues are automatically recovered
Non-Retryable Errors

V2 systems will NOT retry these errors:

  • ❌ Authentication errors (401, 403)
  • ❌ Not found (404)
  • ❌ Bad request (400)
  • ❌ Validation errors (422)
Result: Immediate failure (requires fixing the issue)

Retry Configuration

⚠️ Important: Jenni Logistics uses TWO different retry mechanisms:
  • Immediate Retry (for transient network errors): Fast exponential backoff within seconds
  • Persistent Retry (for failed status updates): Saves to a persistent retry queue and retries over hours
Immediate Retry (Network Errors)
Setting Default Value Description
Max Attempts 3 retries Maximum number of immediate retry attempts
Initial Delay 1 second Delay before first retry
Backoff Strategy Exponential (2x) Each retry waits longer: 1s → 2s → 4s
Max Delay 30 seconds Maximum delay between retries
Connection Timeout 5 seconds Maximum time to establish connection
Read Timeout 30 seconds Maximum time to wait for response
Persistent Retry (Status Updates)

When immediate retries fail, Jenni Logistics saves the update to a persistent retry queue for long-term retry:

Setting Value Description
Max Attempts 3 attempts Maximum number of persistent retry attempts
First Retry 15 minutes First retry scheduled 15 minutes after failure
Second Retry 30 minutes Second retry (if first fails) after 30 minutes
Third Retry 60 minutes Final retry (if second fails) after 60 minutes
Scheduled Job Every 5 minutes Background job checks for pending retries
Expiry 24 hours Updates older than 24 hours marked as expired
Total Window ~2 hours All 3 attempts complete within ~2 hours
How it works: Failed status updates are saved to a persistent retry queue. A scheduled retry worker processes pending updates automatically. This ensures reliable delivery even if your system is temporarily down.

Retry Timeline Examples

Immediate Retry: Network Timeout (Recovers on 2nd Attempt)
15:30:00 | Attempt 1/3 - Sending 5 shipments to YOUR_SYSTEM
15:30:05 | ⚠️ Network timeout - Will retry in 1 second
15:30:06 | Attempt 2/3 - Retrying same request
15:30:08 | ✅ Success! All 5 shipments delivered

Total time: 8 seconds (transparent to user)
Immediate Retry: Server Error (Recovers on 3rd Attempt)
16:00:00 | Attempt 1/3 - Sending 10 shipments
16:00:02 | ⚠️ 503 Service Unavailable - Will retry in 1 second
16:00:03 | Attempt 2/3 - Retrying same request
16:00:05 | ⚠️ 503 Service Unavailable - Will retry in 2 seconds
16:00:07 | Attempt 3/3 - Retrying same request
16:00:09 | ✅ Success! All 10 shipments delivered

Total time: 9 seconds (all immediate retries exhausted but succeeded)
Persistent Retry: Status Update Failure (Your System Down)
14:00:00 | Status Update: Shipment #12345 DELIVERED
14:00:01 | Immediate Retry 1/3: Connection refused
14:00:02 | Immediate Retry 2/3: Connection refused
14:00:04 | Immediate Retry 3/3: Connection refused
14:00:07 | ⚠️ All immediate retries failed
